content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the prompt-generation changes in `src/cli/project.rs` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` and checked the new focused tests. Two robustness gaps remain: oversized planned-elsewhere entries can be silently dropped and then misreported as absent, and oversized dependency entries can cause the dependency section to omit every actual dependency identifier/status.

## Amendments

1. `src/cli/project.rs:1556-1598` can silently drop real planned-elsewhere items when the base line (`id (title) - relationship; status: ...`) alone exceeds the remaining budget. `fit_planned_elsewhere_item_to_budget` returns `None` in that case, `apply_planned_elsewhere_budget` skips the item, and `render_bead_task_prompt` later renders an empty list as `No explicit planned-elsewhere items were supplied.` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:961-964`). A single very long bead title, workstream name, or shared-acceptance relationship can therefore erase genuine planned-elsewhere scope and tell the agent there is none. The budgeter needs to preserve at least a truncated base line or emit an omission marker instead of dropping the item entirely.
   Rationale: This is a correctness issue, not just presentation: it can remove explicit out-of-scope guidance and mislead downstream execution into absorbing work that was already planned elsewhere.
2. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:907-958` drops all concrete dependency details when the first rendered dependency line is longer than `DEPENDENCY_CONTEXT_MAX_BYTES`. In that case the selection loop leaves `selected` empty and returns only an omission bullet like `1 additional upstream dependencies omitted for prompt budget.` That means the prompt can contain no actual dependency id/status/outcome at all, even for a single upstream blocker or downstream dependent with a long title. The renderer should always keep at least the dependency id and statuses, truncating long titles/relationship text instead of replacing the whole section with an omission count.
   Rationale: The bead prompt is supposed to be self-contained and include dependency context. Losing every concrete dependency reference breaks that contract and can leave the implementing agent unaware of the real blockers or dependents.
